steps.label: cause
steps.text: An aromatic white wine is poured into a clean tulip-shaped glass with a bowl wider than its rim.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: The bowl provides surface area for volatile release while the narrower opening directs and concentrates aromas toward the nose.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Delicate primary aromas can be assessed more clearly without requiring an oversized bowl.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: a tulip shape balances aroma release in the bowl with concentration at the rim.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
